---
pagetitle: "Sushovan Majhi"
title: ""
toc: false
page-layout: full
listing:
  - id: dispatches
    contents: dispatches.yaml
    template: ejs/dispatches.ejs
    sort: "date desc"
    page-size: 4
  - id: projects
    contents:
      - research/projects/*.qmd
    type: grid
    grid-columns: 4
    fields: [image, title]
    categories: false
  - id: recent-papers
    contents: research/publications/preprints.yml
    template: ejs/preprints.ejs
    sort: "year desc"
    max-items: 3
  - id: recent-talks
    contents: talks.yml
    template: ejs/recent-talks.ejs
    sort: "date desc"
    page-size: 8
---

```{=html}
<header class="masthead">
  <div class="masthead-row">
    <svg class="masthead-emblem" viewBox="0 0 360 130" role="img" aria-label="Howrah Bridge, Calcutta">
      <title>Howrah Bridge, Calcutta</title>
      <g stroke="currentColor" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
        <!-- LEFT PYLON: slender vertical with peaked top and finial -->
        <g stroke-width="1.0">
          <line x1="58" y1="14" x2="58" y2="106" />
          <line x1="66" y1="14" x2="66" y2="106" />
          <!-- peaked top -->
          <line x1="58" y1="14" x2="62" y2="6" />
          <line x1="66" y1="14" x2="62" y2="6" />
          <line x1="62" y1="6"  x2="62" y2="2" />
          <!-- internal pylon horizontals -->
          <line x1="58" y1="26" x2="66" y2="26" />
          <line x1="58" y1="40" x2="66" y2="40" />
          <line x1="58" y1="54" x2="66" y2="54" />
          <line x1="58" y1="68" x2="66" y2="68" />
          <line x1="58" y1="82" x2="66" y2="82" />
          <line x1="58" y1="94" x2="66" y2="94" />
        </g>
        <!-- LEFT PIER BASE: triangular foundation below deck -->
        <g stroke-width="1.0">
          <line x1="38" y1="120" x2="58" y2="106" />
          <line x1="86" y1="120" x2="66" y2="106" />
          <line x1="38" y1="120" x2="86" y2="120" />
        </g>
        <g stroke-width="0.5">
          <line x1="44" y1="120" x2="62" y2="106" />
          <line x1="80" y1="120" x2="62" y2="106" />
          <line x1="50" y1="115" x2="74" y2="115" />
        </g>
        <!-- RIGHT PYLON (mirror) -->
        <g stroke-width="1.0">
          <line x1="294" y1="14" x2="294" y2="106" />
          <line x1="302" y1="14" x2="302" y2="106" />
          <line x1="294" y1="14" x2="298" y2="6" />
          <line x1="302" y1="14" x2="298" y2="6" />
          <line x1="298" y1="6"  x2="298" y2="2" />
          <line x1="294" y1="26" x2="302" y2="26" />
          <line x1="294" y1="40" x2="302" y2="40" />
          <line x1="294" y1="54" x2="302" y2="54" />
          <line x1="294" y1="68" x2="302" y2="68" />
          <line x1="294" y1="82" x2="302" y2="82" />
          <line x1="294" y1="94" x2="302" y2="94" />
        </g>
        <!-- RIGHT PIER BASE -->
        <g stroke-width="1.0">
          <line x1="274" y1="120" x2="294" y2="106" />
          <line x1="322" y1="120" x2="302" y2="106" />
          <line x1="274" y1="120" x2="322" y2="120" />
        </g>
        <g stroke-width="0.5">
          <line x1="280" y1="120" x2="298" y2="106" />
          <line x1="316" y1="120" x2="298" y2="106" />
          <line x1="286" y1="115" x2="310" y2="115" />
        </g>
        <!-- UPPER CHORD: smooth descending arc from each pylon top to center span -->
        <g stroke-width="1.4">
          <path d="M 66 14 Q 130 22 180 70" />
          <path d="M 180 70 Q 230 22 294 14" />
        </g>
        <!-- LOWER CHORD: horizontal at deck level, end-to-end -->
        <g stroke-width="1.0">
          <line x1="66" y1="98" x2="294" y2="98" />
        </g>
        <!-- MANY VERTICAL POSTS — left half: rising x, descending y to meet upper chord arc -->
        <g stroke-width="0.55">
          <line x1="78"  y1="20" x2="78"  y2="98" />
          <line x1="90"  y1="26" x2="90"  y2="98" />
          <line x1="102" y1="33" x2="102" y2="98" />
          <line x1="114" y1="40" x2="114" y2="98" />
          <line x1="126" y1="48" x2="126" y2="98" />
          <line x1="138" y1="55" x2="138" y2="98" />
          <line x1="150" y1="61" x2="150" y2="98" />
          <line x1="162" y1="66" x2="162" y2="98" />
          <line x1="174" y1="69" x2="174" y2="98" />
          <line x1="186" y1="69" x2="186" y2="98" />
          <line x1="198" y1="66" x2="198" y2="98" />
          <line x1="210" y1="61" x2="210" y2="98" />
          <line x1="222" y1="55" x2="222" y2="98" />
          <line x1="234" y1="48" x2="234" y2="98" />
          <line x1="246" y1="40" x2="246" y2="98" />
          <line x1="258" y1="33" x2="258" y2="98" />
          <line x1="270" y1="26" x2="270" y2="98" />
          <line x1="282" y1="20" x2="282" y2="98" />
        </g>
        <!-- DIAGONAL LATTICE — sloped diagonals between adjacent posts (zig pattern) -->
        <g stroke-width="0.45" opacity="0.85">
          <line x1="66"  y1="14" x2="78"  y2="98" />
          <line x1="78"  y1="20" x2="90"  y2="98" />
          <line x1="90"  y1="26" x2="102" y2="98" />
          <line x1="102" y1="33" x2="114" y2="98" />
          <line x1="114" y1="40" x2="126" y2="98" />
          <line x1="126" y1="48" x2="138" y2="98" />
          <line x1="138" y1="55" x2="150" y2="98" />
          <line x1="150" y1="61" x2="162" y2="98" />
          <line x1="162" y1="66" x2="174" y2="98" />
          <line x1="174" y1="69" x2="180" y2="98" />
          <line x1="180" y1="70" x2="186" y2="98" />
          <line x1="186" y1="69" x2="198" y2="98" />
          <line x1="198" y1="66" x2="210" y2="98" />
          <line x1="210" y1="61" x2="222" y2="98" />
          <line x1="222" y1="55" x2="234" y2="98" />
          <line x1="234" y1="48" x2="246" y2="98" />
          <line x1="246" y1="40" x2="258" y2="98" />
          <line x1="258" y1="33" x2="270" y2="98" />
          <line x1="270" y1="26" x2="282" y2="98" />
          <line x1="282" y1="20" x2="294" y2="98" />
        </g>
        <!-- HORIZONTAL MID-CHORD running along middle of truss for added detail -->
        <g stroke-width="0.45" opacity="0.7">
          <path d="M 66 56 Q 130 60 180 84" />
          <path d="M 180 84 Q 230 60 294 56" />
        </g>
        <!-- DECK -->
        <g stroke-width="1.6">
          <line x1="2" y1="106" x2="358" y2="106" />
        </g>
        <!-- HOOGHLY RIVER ripples -->
        <g stroke-width="0.5" stroke-dasharray="5 3" opacity="0.5">
          <line x1="0"   y1="124" x2="32"  y2="124" />
          <line x1="100" y1="124" x2="270" y2="124" />
          <line x1="328" y1="124" x2="360" y2="124" />
        </g>
      </g>
    </svg>
    <div class="pub-title">Sushovan Majhi</div>
    <svg class="masthead-emblem" viewBox="0 0 240 130" role="img" aria-label="United States Capitol, Washington, D.C.">
      <title>United States Capitol</title>
      <g stroke="currentColor" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
        <!-- STATUE OF FREEDOM atop the dome -->
        <g stroke-width="0.7">
          <circle cx="120" cy="6" r="1.5" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" />
          <line x1="120" y1="7" x2="120" y2="14" />
          <line x1="117" y1="11" x2="123" y2="11" />
        </g>
        <!-- LANTERN (small cupola) -->
        <g stroke-width="0.7">
          <rect x="115" y="15" width="10" height="9" />
          <line x1="120" y1="15" x2="120" y2="24" />
          <line x1="113" y1="24" x2="127" y2="24" />
        </g>
        <!-- MAIN DOME -->
        <g stroke-width="1.2">
          <path d="M 92 56 A 28 32 0 0 1 148 56" />
        </g>
        <!-- dome ribs -->
        <g stroke-width="0.55" opacity="0.85">
          <line x1="120" y1="24" x2="120" y2="56" />
          <line x1="120" y1="24" x2="105" y2="56" />
          <line x1="120" y1="24" x2="135" y2="56" />
          <line x1="120" y1="24" x2="92"  y2="56" />
          <line x1="120" y1="24" x2="148" y2="56" />
          <line x1="120" y1="24" x2="98"  y2="56" />
          <line x1="120" y1="24" x2="142" y2="56" />
          <line x1="120" y1="24" x2="112" y2="56" />
          <line x1="120" y1="24" x2="128" y2="56" />
        </g>
        <!-- horizontal latitude curves on dome -->
        <g stroke-width="0.4" opacity="0.6">
          <path d="M 99 35 Q 120 31 141 35" />
          <path d="M 95 45 Q 120 41 145 45" />
        </g>
        <!-- COLONNADE (peristyle) around base of dome -->
        <g stroke-width="0.7">
          <line x1="88" y1="56" x2="152" y2="56" />
          <line x1="88" y1="68" x2="152" y2="68" />
          <line x1="88" y1="56" x2="88" y2="68" />
          <line x1="152" y1="56" x2="152" y2="68" />
          <line x1="94"  y1="56" x2="94"  y2="68" />
          <line x1="100" y1="56" x2="100" y2="68" />
          <line x1="106" y1="56" x2="106" y2="68" />
          <line x1="112" y1="56" x2="112" y2="68" />
          <line x1="118" y1="56" x2="118" y2="68" />
          <line x1="122" y1="56" x2="122" y2="68" />
          <line x1="128" y1="56" x2="128" y2="68" />
          <line x1="134" y1="56" x2="134" y2="68" />
          <line x1="140" y1="56" x2="140" y2="68" />
          <line x1="146" y1="56" x2="146" y2="68" />
        </g>
        <!-- LOWER DRUM with arched windows -->
        <g stroke-width="0.7">
          <line x1="84" y1="68" x2="156" y2="68" />
          <line x1="84" y1="84" x2="156" y2="84" />
          <line x1="84" y1="68" x2="84" y2="84" />
          <line x1="156" y1="68" x2="156" y2="84" />
          <path d="M 90 84 L 90 76 A 4 4 0 0 1 98 76 L 98 84" />
          <path d="M 104 84 L 104 76 A 4 4 0 0 1 112 76 L 112 84" />
          <path d="M 116 84 L 116 76 A 4 4 0 0 1 124 76 L 124 84" />
          <path d="M 128 84 L 128 76 A 4 4 0 0 1 136 76 L 136 84" />
          <path d="M 142 84 L 142 76 A 4 4 0 0 1 150 76 L 150 84" />
        </g>
        <!-- PEDIMENT and rosette -->
        <g stroke-width="0.9">
          <path d="M 72 96 L 120 84 L 168 96 Z" />
          <line x1="72" y1="96" x2="168" y2="96" />
          <circle cx="120" cy="92" r="1.5" />
        </g>
        <!-- COLUMNS (Corinthian, eight) -->
        <g stroke-width="0.7">
          <line x1="80"  y1="96" x2="80"  y2="114" />
          <line x1="92"  y1="96" x2="92"  y2="114" />
          <line x1="104" y1="96" x2="104" y2="114" />
          <line x1="114" y1="96" x2="114" y2="114" />
          <line x1="126" y1="96" x2="126" y2="114" />
          <line x1="136" y1="96" x2="136" y2="114" />
          <line x1="148" y1="96" x2="148" y2="114" />
          <line x1="160" y1="96" x2="160" y2="114" />
          <!-- capital marks -->
          <line x1="78"  y1="98" x2="82"  y2="98" />
          <line x1="90"  y1="98" x2="94"  y2="98" />
          <line x1="102" y1="98" x2="106" y2="98" />
          <line x1="112" y1="98" x2="116" y2="98" />
          <line x1="124" y1="98" x2="128" y2="98" />
          <line x1="134" y1="98" x2="138" y2="98" />
          <line x1="146" y1="98" x2="150" y2="98" />
          <line x1="158" y1="98" x2="162" y2="98" />
        </g>
        <!-- STEPS in front -->
        <g stroke-width="0.8">
          <line x1="68" y1="114" x2="172" y2="114" />
          <line x1="64" y1="118" x2="176" y2="118" />
          <line x1="60" y1="122" x2="180" y2="122" />
        </g>
        <!-- WINGS -->
        <g stroke-width="0.7">
          <line x1="14" y1="100" x2="72" y2="100" />
          <line x1="14" y1="100" x2="14" y2="118" />
          <line x1="14" y1="118" x2="72" y2="118" />
          <line x1="22" y1="102" x2="22" y2="118" />
          <line x1="32" y1="102" x2="32" y2="118" />
          <line x1="42" y1="102" x2="42" y2="118" />
          <line x1="52" y1="102" x2="52" y2="118" />
          <line x1="62" y1="102" x2="62" y2="118" />
          <line x1="14" y1="106" x2="72" y2="106" />
          <line x1="14" y1="112" x2="72" y2="112" />
          <line x1="168" y1="100" x2="226" y2="100" />
          <line x1="226" y1="100" x2="226" y2="118" />
          <line x1="168" y1="118" x2="226" y2="118" />
          <line x1="178" y1="102" x2="178" y2="118" />
          <line x1="188" y1="102" x2="188" y2="118" />
          <line x1="198" y1="102" x2="198" y2="118" />
          <line x1="208" y1="102" x2="208" y2="118" />
          <line x1="218" y1="102" x2="218" y2="118" />
          <line x1="168" y1="106" x2="226" y2="106" />
          <line x1="168" y1="112" x2="226" y2="112" />
        </g>
        <!-- GROUND -->
        <g stroke-width="1.2">
          <line x1="6" y1="125" x2="234" y2="125" />
        </g>
      </g>
    </svg>
  </div>
  <div class="edition">
    <span>Assistant Professor of Data Science</span>
    <span>The George Washington University</span>
    <span>Samson Hall 313, Washington, D.C.</span>
    <span><a href="mailto:s.majhi@gwu.edu">s.majhi@gwu.edu</a></span>
    <span>(+1) 202-994-1235</span>
  </div>
  <svg class="trajectory-map" viewBox="0 0 360 56" role="img" aria-label="Five cities of the author's trajectory">
    <defs><style>
      .traj-arc { stroke: currentColor; fill: none; stroke-width: 0.6; stroke-dasharray: 3 3; opacity: 0.5; }
      .traj-dot { fill: currentColor; }
      .traj-label { font-family: 'EB Garamond', 'Alegreya', Georgia, serif; font-style: italic; font-size: 9px; fill: currentColor; opacity: 0.7; }
    </style></defs>
    <path class="traj-arc" d="M 30 22 Q 67 8 105 22 Q 142 36 180 22 Q 217 8 255 22 Q 292 36 330 22" />
    <g class="traj-dot">
      <circle cx="30"  cy="22" r="2.6" />
      <circle cx="105" cy="22" r="2.6" />
      <circle cx="180" cy="22" r="2.6" />
      <circle cx="255" cy="22" r="2.6" />
      <circle cx="330" cy="22" r="2.6" />
    </g>
    <g class="traj-label" text-anchor="middle">
      <text x="30"  y="42">Calcutta</text>
      <text x="105" y="42">Bangalore</text>
      <text x="180" y="42">New Orleans</text>
      <text x="255" y="42">Berkeley</text>
      <text x="330" y="42">Washington</text>
    </g>
  </svg>
</header>

<section class="hero-frontispiece">
  <figure class="hero-figure plate">
    <img src="assets/me-plate-alt.jpg" alt="Portrait of Sushovan Majhi.">
    <figcaption><em>From Kawaguchiko, in the summer of MMXXV.</em></figcaption>
  </figure>
  <div class="hero-text">
    <div class="hero-bio">
      <p>What is the shape of this thing? Topology's oldest question&mdash;and one that statistics has been almost-asking for a century. Posed correctly, it turns out to be the right question for data more often than is generally conceded.</p>
      <p>I am a mathematician&mdash;Calcutta, Bangalore, New Orleans, Berkeley, now Washington&mdash;collecting, with each city, a different lesson in how shape is recovered from noise. The papers make those lessons rigorous: when a Vietoris&ndash;Rips complex remembers the manifold it was sampled from, when a Reeb graph can be talked into reconstructing a road network from GPS traces, and on those rare occasions when a Gromov&ndash;Hausdorff distance can be talked into being computed at all. The collaborations export the proofs to finance, climate, fluid mechanics, and biology&mdash;disciplines where the data is unrepentantly real.</p>
      <p><em>Open to advising the curious and the obstinate. <a href="mailto:s.majhi@gwu.edu">Write</a>.</em></p>
    </div>
    <nav class="hero-links">
      <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=3zP2QQsAAAAJ&amp;hl=en&amp;oi=ao">Google&nbsp;Scholar</a>
      <a href="https://github.com/sushovan4">GitHub</a>
      <a href="https://linkedin.com/in/smajhi">LinkedIn</a>
      <a href="assets/cv.pdf">Curriculum&nbsp;Vit&aelig;</a>
    </nav>
  </div>
</section>
```

::: {.dispatches}
::: {.dispatches-title}
Dispatches from the Field
:::

::: {#dispatches .dispatches-grid}
:::
:::

## Research

```{=html}
<div class="section-byline">
  <span>Filed under <em>Research Programme</em></span>
  <span>Updated MMXXVI</span>
</div>
<p class="section-kicker">Two halves of one programme: the theorems and the data that asked the question.</p>
```

Two halves of the mathematical foundations of data science, with topology as the lens. On one side, theorems about when a finite sample remembers the shape it was drawn from. On the other, the disreputable data that wanted to know in the first place. The two halves keep each other honest&mdash;and, on bad days, mutually embarrassed.

The theoretical side develops provable methods for shape, graph, and manifold reconstruction. The objects of study are simplicial complexes built from finite samples&mdash;Vietoris&ndash;Rips, &Ccaron;ech, alpha&mdash;and the questions are about when, and how faithfully, such complexes recover the topology and geometry of an unknown ground truth. The tools are inherited from algebraic topology, metric geometry, and computational geometry; all three are considerably older than the data they have lately been asked to analyse.

```{=html}
<div class="pull-quote">
  &ldquo;The theorems decide when, and how faithfully, a finite sample remembers the manifold it was drawn from.&rdquo;
</div>

<figure class="plate-spread">
  <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 560 280" role="img" aria-label="The pipeline: data, topology, descriptor, classifier" class="frontispiece">
    <defs><style>
      .ink { stroke: currentColor; fill: none; stroke-width: 1.2; stroke-linecap: round; stroke-linejoin: round; }
      .hair { stroke: currentColor; fill: none; stroke-width: 0.5; stroke-dasharray: 2 2; opacity: 0.55; }
      .axis { stroke: currentColor; fill: none; stroke-width: 0.7; opacity: 0.65; }
      .diag { stroke: currentColor; fill: none; stroke-width: 0.7; stroke-dasharray: 3 2; opacity: 0.5; }
      .curve { stroke: currentColor; fill: none; stroke-width: 1.2; opacity: 0.85; stroke-linecap: round; stroke-linejoin: round; }
      .hyperplane { stroke: currentColor; fill: none; stroke-width: 1.2; opacity: 0.85; }
      .vertex { fill: currentColor; stroke: none; }
      .vertex-open { fill: none; stroke: currentColor; stroke-width: 1.1; }
      .landmark { fill: none; stroke: currentColor; stroke-width: 1.4; }
      .triangle { fill: currentColor; opacity: 0.10; stroke: currentColor; stroke-width: 0.8; stroke-linejoin: round; }
      .border { stroke: currentColor; fill: none; stroke-width: 0.8; opacity: 0.4; }
      .arrow { font-family: 'EB Garamond', Georgia, serif; font-size: 18px; fill: currentColor; opacity: 0.5; }
      .panel-label { font-family: 'EB Garamond', Georgia, serif; font-style: italic; font-size: 9px; fill: currentColor; opacity: 0.6; letter-spacing: 0.04em; }
      .sub-label { font-family: 'EB Garamond', Georgia, serif; font-style: italic; font-size: 7.5px; fill: currentColor; opacity: 0.55; letter-spacing: 0.03em; }
      .plate-label { font-family: 'EB Garamond', Georgia, serif; font-style: italic; font-size: 10px; fill: currentColor; opacity: 0.65; }
      .axis-label { font-family: 'EB Garamond', Georgia, serif; font-style: italic; font-size: 7px; fill: currentColor; opacity: 0.65; }
    </style></defs>
    <rect class="border" x="6" y="6" width="548" height="268" />
    <rect class="border" x="11" y="11" width="538" height="258" />

    <line class="hair" x1="144" y1="24" x2="144" y2="256" />
    <line class="hair" x1="282" y1="24" x2="282" y2="256" />
    <line class="hair" x1="420" y1="24" x2="420" y2="256" />

    <text class="panel-label" x="75"  y="32" text-anchor="middle">the data</text>
    <text class="panel-label" x="213" y="32" text-anchor="middle">the topology</text>
    <text class="panel-label" x="351" y="32" text-anchor="middle">the descriptor</text>
    <text class="panel-label" x="487" y="32" text-anchor="middle">the classifier</text>

    <text class="arrow" x="144" y="148" text-anchor="middle">&rarr;</text>
    <text class="arrow" x="282" y="148" text-anchor="middle">&rarr;</text>
    <text class="arrow" x="420" y="148" text-anchor="middle">&rarr;</text>

    <!-- PANEL 1: data—top: point cloud; bottom: graph -->
    <text class="sub-label" x="14" y="48">point cloud</text>
    <g class="vertex">
      <circle cx="45"  cy="60"  r="2.8" /><circle cx="70"  cy="70"  r="2.8" /><circle cx="95"  cy="62"  r="2.8" />
      <circle cx="118" cy="78"  r="2.8" /><circle cx="120" cy="100" r="2.8" /><circle cx="108" cy="120" r="2.8" />
      <circle cx="80"  cy="128" r="2.8" /><circle cx="55"  cy="125" r="2.8" /><circle cx="32"  cy="108" r="2.8" />
      <circle cx="28"  cy="80"  r="2.8" />
    </g>
    <text class="sub-label" x="14" y="164">graph</text>
    <g class="ink">
      <line x1="40"  y1="180" x2="78"  y2="172" />
      <line x1="78"  y1="172" x2="115" y2="186" />
      <line x1="115" y1="186" x2="118" y2="218" />
      <line x1="118" y1="218" x2="92"  y2="240" />
      <line x1="92"  y1="240" x2="55"  y2="240" />
      <line x1="55"  y1="240" x2="28"  y2="216" />
      <line x1="28"  y1="216" x2="40"  y2="180" />
      <line x1="78"  y1="205" x2="78"  y2="172" />
      <line x1="78"  y1="205" x2="118" y2="218" />
      <line x1="78"  y1="205" x2="55"  y2="240" />
    </g>
    <g class="vertex">
      <circle cx="40"  cy="180" r="2.8" /><circle cx="78"  cy="172" r="2.8" /><circle cx="115" cy="186" r="2.8" />
      <circle cx="118" cy="218" r="2.8" /><circle cx="92"  cy="240" r="2.8" /><circle cx="55"  cy="240" r="2.8" />
      <circle cx="28"  cy="216" r="2.8" /><circle cx="78"  cy="205" r="2.8" />
    </g>

    <!-- PANEL 2: topology—top: Vietoris–Rips; bottom: alpha -->
    <text class="sub-label" x="152" y="48">Rips</text>
    <circle class="hair" cx="213" cy="85" r="32" />
    <polygon class="triangle" points="245,85 240,101 227,113" />
    <polygon class="triangle" points="199,113 186,101 181,85" />
    <polygon class="triangle" points="181,85 186,69 199,57" />
    <polygon class="triangle" points="213,53 227,57 240,69" />
    <g class="ink">
      <line x1="245" y1="85"  x2="240" y2="101" /><line x1="240" y1="101" x2="227" y2="113" />
      <line x1="227" y1="113" x2="213" y2="117" /><line x1="213" y1="117" x2="199" y2="113" />
      <line x1="199" y1="113" x2="186" y2="101" /><line x1="186" y1="101" x2="181" y2="85"  />
      <line x1="181" y1="85"  x2="186" y2="69"  /><line x1="186" y1="69"  x2="199" y2="57"  />
      <line x1="199" y1="57"  x2="213" y2="53"  /><line x1="213" y1="53"  x2="227" y2="57"  />
      <line x1="227" y1="57"  x2="240" y2="69"  /><line x1="240" y1="69"  x2="245" y2="85"  />
      <line x1="245" y1="85"  x2="227" y2="113" /><line x1="199" y1="113" x2="181" y2="85" />
      <line x1="181" y1="85"  x2="199" y2="57"  /><line x1="213" y1="53"  x2="240" y2="69" />
    </g>
    <g class="vertex">
      <circle cx="245" cy="85"  r="2.4" /><circle cx="240" cy="101" r="2.4" /><circle cx="227" cy="113" r="2.4" />
      <circle cx="213" cy="117" r="2.4" /><circle cx="199" cy="113" r="2.4" /><circle cx="186" cy="101" r="2.4" />
      <circle cx="181" cy="85"  r="2.4" /><circle cx="186" cy="69"  r="2.4" /><circle cx="199" cy="57"  r="2.4" />
      <circle cx="213" cy="53"  r="2.4" /><circle cx="227" cy="57"  r="2.4" /><circle cx="240" cy="69"  r="2.4" />
    </g>
    <g class="landmark">
      <circle cx="245" cy="85"  r="5.5" />
      <circle cx="181" cy="85"  r="5.5" />
    </g>

    <text class="sub-label" x="152" y="164">alpha</text>
    <circle class="hair" cx="213" cy="200" r="32" />
    <g class="ink">
      <line x1="245" y1="200" x2="240" y2="216" /><line x1="240" y1="216" x2="227" y2="228" />
      <line x1="227" y1="228" x2="213" y2="232" /><line x1="213" y1="232" x2="199" y2="228" />
      <line x1="199" y1="228" x2="186" y2="216" /><line x1="186" y1="216" x2="181" y2="200" />
      <line x1="181" y1="200" x2="186" y2="184" /><line x1="186" y1="184" x2="199" y2="172" />
      <line x1="199" y1="172" x2="213" y2="168" /><line x1="213" y1="168" x2="227" y2="172" />
      <line x1="227" y1="172" x2="240" y2="184" /><line x1="240" y1="184" x2="245" y2="200" />
    </g>
    <g class="vertex">
      <circle cx="245" cy="200" r="2.4" /><circle cx="240" cy="216" r="2.4" /><circle cx="227" cy="228" r="2.4" />
      <circle cx="213" cy="232" r="2.4" /><circle cx="199" cy="228" r="2.4" /><circle cx="186" cy="216" r="2.4" />
      <circle cx="181" cy="200" r="2.4" /><circle cx="186" cy="184" r="2.4" /><circle cx="199" cy="172" r="2.4" />
      <circle cx="213" cy="168" r="2.4" /><circle cx="227" cy="172" r="2.4" /><circle cx="240" cy="184" r="2.4" />
    </g>
    <g class="landmark">
      <circle cx="213" cy="232" r="5.5" />
      <circle cx="213" cy="168" r="5.5" />
    </g>

    <!-- PANEL 3: descriptor—PD (top) and ECS surface (bottom) -->
    <text class="sub-label" x="290" y="48">PD</text>
    <g class="axis">
      <line x1="300" y1="138" x2="393" y2="138" />
      <line x1="300" y1="138" x2="300" y2="45" />
    </g>
    <line class="diag" x1="300" y1="138" x2="393" y2="45" />
    <text class="axis-label" x="298" y="44"  text-anchor="end">d</text>
    <text class="axis-label" x="393" y="150" text-anchor="end">b</text>
    <g class="vertex">
      <circle cx="315" cy="65" r="3.4" />
      <circle cx="340" cy="90" r="2.6" />
      <circle cx="365" cy="60" r="2.4" />
    </g>
    <circle class="landmark" cx="315" cy="65" r="6.5" />

    <text class="sub-label" x="290" y="166">ECS</text>
    <text class="axis-label" x="298" y="186" text-anchor="end">τ</text>
    <text class="axis-label" x="412" y="248" text-anchor="end">ε</text>
    <g class="axis">
      <line x1="300" y1="240" x2="410" y2="240" />
      <line x1="300" y1="240" x2="300" y2="180" />
    </g>
    <!-- ECS heatmap: 6 cols x 4 rows of cells, fill-opacity encodes χ(ε,τ) -->
    <g fill="currentColor" stroke="none">
      <rect x="305" y="180" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.06" /><rect x="319" y="180" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.10" /><rect x="333" y="180" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.22" /><rect x="347" y="180" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.42" /><rect x="361" y="180" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.55" /><rect x="375" y="180" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.46" />
      <rect x="305" y="194" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.10" /><rect x="319" y="194" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.22" /><rect x="333" y="194" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.42" /><rect x="347" y="194" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.62" /><rect x="361" y="194" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.46" /><rect x="375" y="194" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.28" />
      <rect x="305" y="208" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.22" /><rect x="319" y="208" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.42" /><rect x="333" y="208" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.55" /><rect x="347" y="208" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.42" /><rect x="361" y="208" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.26" /><rect x="375" y="208" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.14" />
      <rect x="305" y="222" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.36" /><rect x="319" y="222" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.50" /><rect x="333" y="222" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.42" /><rect x="347" y="222" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.26" /><rect x="361" y="222" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.14" /><rect x="375" y="222" width="14" height="14" fill-opacity="0.08" />
    </g>

    <!-- PANEL 4: classifier—separating hyperplane in embedding space -->
    <g class="axis">
      <rect x="438" y="80" width="106" height="142" stroke="currentColor" fill="none" stroke-width="0.5" opacity="0.35" />
    </g>
    <line class="hyperplane" x1="445" y1="210" x2="538" y2="92" />
    <g class="vertex">
      <circle cx="455" cy="100" r="2.6" />
      <circle cx="472" cy="118" r="2.6" />
      <circle cx="468" cy="138" r="2.6" />
      <circle cx="495" cy="105" r="2.6" />
      <circle cx="485" cy="130" r="2.6" />
      <circle cx="510" cy="118" r="2.6" />
    </g>
    <g class="vertex-open">
      <circle cx="468" cy="195" r="2.8" />
      <circle cx="490" cy="180" r="2.8" />
      <circle cx="510" cy="200" r="2.8" />
      <circle cx="520" cy="170" r="2.8" />
      <circle cx="530" cy="195" r="2.8" />
      <circle cx="500" cy="215" r="2.8" />
    </g>

    <text class="plate-label" x="548" y="262" text-anchor="end">Plate&nbsp;I</text>
  </svg>
  <figcaption><b>The pipeline in four panels</b>&mdash;data (point cloud or graph) to topology (Vietoris&ndash;Rips or alpha), to descriptor (persistence diagram or Euler characteristic surface), to a classifier in embedding space. Landmarks are ringed where they appear.</figcaption>
</figure>
```

The applied side carries the methods into finance, climate, fluid mechanics, and biology&mdash;wherever the data is unrepentantly high-dimensional but is suspected, often correctly, of living on something simpler. Recent collaborations have hunted monsoon onsets, the topology of the polar vortex, two-phase flow regimes, and the moods of the stock market.

Seven projects, in summary. Click a card for collaborators, papers, and the long-form excuse for the title.

:::{#projects}
:::

The full programme&mdash;projects, publications, and ongoing collaborations&mdash;lives on the [research page](research/).

## Recent Papers

```{=html}
<div class="section-byline">
  <span>Filed under <em>Preprints</em></span>
  <span>Most recent three</span>
  <span>Updated MMXXVI</span>
</div>
<p class="section-kicker">In the order they have most recently surprised their author.</p>
```

The three most recent preprints, listed in the order in which they have most recently surprised their author. The full account&mdash;journals, conference proceedings, and the thesis&mdash;is on the [publications page](research/publications/).

:::{#recent-papers}
:::

## Students and Mentees

```{=html}
<div class="section-byline">
  <span>Filed under <em>The Group</em></span>
  <span>The George Washington University</span>
  <span>2025&ndash;26</span>
</div>
<p class="section-kicker">Eight, between Washington, Sikkim, and Zurich.</p>
```

Eight students between GWU, NIT Sikkim, and Zurich. Each has been issued a corner of the programme&mdash;kernels, descriptors, reconstruction, dynamics&mdash;and instructed to send dispatches.

```{=html}
<aside class="advising-notice">
  <div class="ad-headline">Wanted</div>
  <div class="ad-subhead">Graduate &amp; Undergraduate Students</div>
  <div class="ad-body">
    <p>For a research programme in <em>applied topology</em>, <em>computational geometry</em>, and the <em>mathematical foundations of machine learning</em>. Required: stamina for definitions, suspicion of fashion, and a willingness to argue at some length about whether the boundary belongs to the set.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="ad-signoff">
    <span class="ad-finger">&#9758;</span>
    Apply by post: <a href="mailto:s.majhi@gwu.edu">s.majhi@gwu.edu</a>
  </div>
</aside>

<div class="roster">

<h4>Doctoral students</h4>
<ul class="roster-list">
  <li>
    <span class="r-name">Buddha Nath Sharma</span>
    <span class="r-affil">Ph.D., NIT Sikkim</span>
    <span class="r-topic"><a href="research/projects/dynamics.html">Topological data analysis for time series and dynamical systems.</a></span>
    <span class="r-meta">Co-advised with Md.&nbsp;Nurujjaman.</span>
  </li>
</ul>

<h4>Master's students</h4>
<ul class="roster-list">
  <li>
    <span class="r-name">Alexander D. Silberman, Chinaza Belolisa, Madeline Bumpus</span>
    <span class="r-affil">M.S. Data Science, GWU</span>
    <span class="r-topic"><a href="research/projects/recsys.html">Topological methods for recommender systems.</a></span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="r-name">Sayam Palrecha</span>
    <span class="r-affil">M.S. Data Science, GWU</span>
    <span class="r-topic"><a href="research/projects/dynamics.html">Topological data analysis for finance.</a></span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="r-name">James Moukheiber</span>
    <span class="r-affil">M.S., University of Zurich</span>
    <span class="r-topic"><a href="research/projects/graph-recon.html">Topological data analysis for geographic information systems.</a></span>
  </li>
</ul>

<h4>Undergraduate students</h4>
<ul class="roster-list">
  <li>
    <span class="r-name">Edward Bae</span>
    <span class="r-affil">B.S. Computer Science, GWU</span>
    <span class="r-topic"><a href="research/projects/recsys.html">Topological methods for recommender systems.</a></span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="r-name">Abby Stein</span>
    <span class="r-affil">B.S. Data Science, GWU</span>
    <span class="r-topic"><a href="research/projects/dynamics.html">Topological methods for petroleum flow analysis.</a></span>
  </li>
</ul>

<h4>Past mentees</h4>
<ul class="roster-list">
  <li>
    <span class="r-name">Khush Shah, Shikha Kumari</span>
    <span class="r-affil">M.S. Data Science, GWU</span>
    <span class="r-topic"><a href="research/projects/graph-recon.html">Geometric graph reconstruction.</a></span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="r-name">Anish Rai</span>
    <span class="r-affil">Ph.D., NIT Sikkim</span>
    <span class="r-topic"><a href="research/projects/dynamics.html">Topological data analysis for finance.</a></span>
  </li>
</ul>

</div>
```

## Teaching

```{=html}
<p class="section-kicker">Foundations to topology, four institutions, three countries.</p>
```

Foundations to topology, taught at four institutions across three countries. The students change; the limits do not.

*Currently teaching* **Algorithm Design for Data Science** *and the* **Undergraduate Capstone** *at GWU, Spring 2026.* The full record is on the [teaching page](teaching/); the [teaching statement](assets/teaching-statement.pdf) carries the philosophy &mdash; or, failing that, the alibi.

## Service

```{=html}
<div class="section-byline">
  <span>Filed under <em>Department</em></span>
  <span>2014&ndash;present</span>
</div>
<p class="section-kicker">The unpaid half of the profession.</p>
```

Editorial and committee work&mdash;the unpaid half of the profession, and the half in which the field actually gets organised.

```{=html}
<ul class="roster-list">
  <li>
    <span class="r-name">Undergraduate Advisor</span>
    <span class="r-affil">GWU Data Science · 2024–current</span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="r-name">AMS Special Session co-organiser</span>
    <span class="r-affil">JMM Washington, D.C. · 2026 · Seattle 2025 · AMS Fall Sectional Tulane 2025</span>
    <span class="r-topic">Sessions on <em>Topological Data Analysis for Non-linear Dynamics</em>, <em>Topological and Geometric Shape Reconstruction</em>, <em>Climate Science at the Interface between TDA and Dynamical Systems</em>, and <a href="https://www.ams.org/meetings/sectional/2328_program_ss24.html"><em>Applied Topology and Topological Data Analysis</em></a>.</span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="r-name">Ph.D. thesis reader</span>
    <span class="r-affil">Tulane 2024 · GWU Mathematics 2025</span>
    <span class="r-topic">Will Tran, <em>Distortion and Curvature in the Shape Reconstruction Problem</em>; Peiqi Yang, <em>Stochastic Approximation on Manifolds and Topological Data Analysis</em>.</span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="r-name">Journal &amp; conference reviewer</span>
    <span class="r-affil">SIMODS · DCG · FoDS · SoCG · WADS · others</span>
    <span class="r-topic">Reviewed for <em>SIAM Journal on Mathematics of Data Science</em>, <em>Discrete and Computational Geometry</em>, <em>Foundations of Data Science</em>, the <em>Symposium on Computational Geometry</em>, and several others.</span>
  </li>
</ul>
```

The complete ledger&mdash;including the SIAM Graduate Student Chapter at Tulane and the data-science webinar series at UC Berkeley&mdash;has been quietly preserved in the [CV](assets/cv.pdf).

## Talks

```{=html}
<p class="section-kicker">Newest first, on the assumption that the most recent argument is the one most likely still to hold.</p>
```

Invited talks, conferences, and seminars.

:::{#recent-talks .compact-list}
:::

## Education

```{=html}
<p class="section-kicker">Calcutta, Bangalore, New Orleans &mdash; and a Berkeley coda.</p>
```

Calcutta, Bangalore, New Orleans &mdash; fourteen years of mathematics across three institutions, with a Berkeley postdoctoral coda for the slow art of saying it plainly.

```{=html}
<ul class="roster-list">
  <li>
    <span class="r-name">Ph.D. in Mathematics</span>
    <span class="r-affil">Tulane &middot; New Orleans</span>
    <span class="r-topic">Advised by Carola Wenk. Computational geometry, computational topology, topological data analysis, differential geometry.</span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="r-name">M.S. in Mathematics</span>
    <span class="r-affil">TIFR &middot; Bangalore</span>
    <span class="r-topic">Differential equations, probability theory, complex and functional analysis, measure theory.</span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="r-name">B.S. (Hons.) in Mathematics</span>
    <span class="r-affil">Ramakrishna Mission Vidyamandira &middot; Calcutta University</span>
    <span class="r-topic">Real analysis, linear algebra, numerical analysis, statistics, physics.</span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="r-name">Postdoctoral Research Fellow</span>
    <span class="r-affil">UC Berkeley &middot; School of Information</span>
    <span class="r-topic">Data science research and the MIDS lecture series.</span>
  </li>
</ul>

<div class="wax-seal-row" aria-hidden="true">
  <svg class="wax-seal" viewBox="0 0 80 80" role="img" aria-label="Author's seal">
    <defs>
      <style>
        .seal-ring  { stroke: currentColor; fill: none; stroke-width: 1.3; }
        .seal-inner { stroke: currentColor; fill: none; stroke-width: 0.45; }
        .seal-mark  { fill: currentColor; stroke: none; }
        .seal-thin  { stroke: currentColor; fill: none; stroke-width: 0.55; }
        .seal-mono  { font-family: 'Noto Serif Bengali', 'Bengali Sangam MN', 'Kalpurush', 'Vrinda', 'Lohit Bengali', 'Sagar', 'Akaash', serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 12px; fill: currentColor; }
        .seal-band  { font-family: 'Alegreya SC', 'EB Garamond', Georgia, serif; font-size: 4.6px; letter-spacing: 0.22em; fill: currentColor; }
      </style>
      <path id="seal-top"    d="M 11 40 A 29 29 0 0 1 69 40" />
      <path id="seal-bottom" d="M 11 40 A 29 29 0 0 0 69 40" />
    </defs>
    <!-- nested rings -->
    <circle class="seal-ring"  cx="40" cy="40" r="36" />
    <circle class="seal-inner" cx="40" cy="40" r="33" />
    <circle class="seal-inner" cx="40" cy="40" r="22" />
    <!-- circular text band: name above, year below -->
    <text class="seal-band">
      <textPath href="#seal-top" startOffset="50%" text-anchor="middle">SVSHOVAN  MAJHI</textPath>
    </text>
    <text class="seal-band">
      <textPath href="#seal-bottom" startOffset="50%" text-anchor="middle">·  ANNO  MMXXVI  ·</textPath>
    </text>
    <!-- Vietoris–Rips complex (kite): the bottom vertex rests on the matra of সুশোভন below,
         visually anchoring the topological motif to the name. -->
    <g>
      <polygon class="seal-mark" opacity="0.16" points="35,33 40,24 40,41" />
      <polygon class="seal-mark" opacity="0.16" points="40,24 45,33 40,41" />
      <line class="seal-thin" x1="35" y1="33" x2="40" y2="24" />
      <line class="seal-thin" x1="40" y1="24" x2="45" y2="33" />
      <line class="seal-thin" x1="35" y1="33" x2="40" y2="41" />
      <line class="seal-thin" x1="45" y1="33" x2="40" y2="41" />
      <line class="seal-thin" x1="40" y1="24" x2="40" y2="41" />
      <line class="seal-thin" x1="35" y1="33" x2="45" y2="33" />
      <circle class="seal-mark" cx="35" cy="33" r="0.95" />
      <circle class="seal-mark" cx="40" cy="24" r="0.95" />
      <circle class="seal-mark" cx="45" cy="33" r="0.95" />
      <circle class="seal-mark" cx="40" cy="41" r="0.95" />
    </g>
    <!-- Full first name in Bengali; matra naturally aligns with VR's lowest vertex above -->
    <text class="seal-mono" x="40" y="51" text-anchor="middle">সুশোভন</text>
    <!-- Asterism (three dots arranged as a 0-skeleton of a 2-simplex), echoing the VR motif -->
    <g class="seal-mark">
      <circle cx="40" cy="56" r="0.6" />
      <circle cx="37" cy="59" r="0.6" />
      <circle cx="43" cy="59" r="0.6" />
    </g>
    <!-- band-separator pearls at 9 / 3 o'clock -->
    <circle class="seal-mark" cx="9"  cy="40" r="0.95" />
    <circle class="seal-mark" cx="71" cy="40" r="0.95" />
    <!-- corner pin-marks -->
    <g class="seal-mark">
      <circle cx="14" cy="14" r="0.55" />
      <circle cx="66" cy="14" r="0.55" />
      <circle cx="14" cy="66" r="0.55" />
      <circle cx="66" cy="66" r="0.55" />
    </g>
  </svg>
</div>
```
